title: Sneaky warning: 'alcohol beverages' instead of 'alcoholic beverages' in first sentence (blue-cast, level 10, upside-down)
image_prompt: Photorealistic clear glass bottle with a red ship sail label. Verbatim text to include exactly: 'Crimson Sail', 'Rum', '40% Alc./Vol.', '750 mL', 'Distilled and bottled by Crimson Distillers, Key West, FL'. Include a separate paragraph in small print: 'GOVERNMENT WARNING: (1) According to the Surgeon General, women should not drink alcohol beverages during pregnancy because of the risk of birth defects. (2) Consumption of alcoholic beverages impairs your ability to drive a car or operate machinery, and may cause health problems.'
visualObservation: Visual note: label photo has a severe blue phone-camera color cast. Camera orientation is upside-down (180 degrees). The photo is upside down and needs rotation before automated approval.
